question: What is the volume of the Core Region?

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software and manual calculations. It accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and design requirements, ensuring efficient operation, safety margins, and reliable natural circulation with minimal pressure losses in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and it incorporates systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity. The system interfaces with several other subsystems, ensuring effective operation and containment of radionuclides.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering unnecessary reactor trips or safety system actuations. Instrumentation provides continuous monitoring of pressure and water level to support safe operation.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

edges.description: Section B describes the pressurizer as being located above the region of naturally circulating reactor coolant, which indirectly defines the upper boundary of the core region referenced in Section A's system evaluation.

Section A discusses the evaluation of system performance parameters, while Section B provides the general design basis and components of the reactor coolant system, with both sections referencing the primary coolant and reactor core region as part of their descriptions.

Section B specifies that the pressurizer is the upper region of the reactor vessel above the core region, which is separated by a baffle plate, thus providing structural context for identifying the core region's volume referenced in the analysis question.

Section A describes system features and operational details of the reactor coolant system, while Section B lists references that provide supporting standards and guidelines for the integrity and quality assurance of the reactor coolant boundary, connecting through the regulatory and technical basis for the system described in Section A.

Section A describes specific features of the reactor coolant system (RCS), such as degasification and pressure boundary integrity, while Section B provides a general overview of the RCS components and their functions, with both sections referencing the RCS as the connecting information.

Section A describes the pressurizer as the upper region of the reactor vessel, separated from the core region by a baffle plate, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system including the reactor pressure vessel and its subsystems, thus Section A specifies the physical relationship and separation between the pressurizer and the core region referenced in Section B.

Section A lists references cited in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B provides a summary description of the reactor coolant system design, with Section A referencing documents that may support or provide background for the information summarized in Section B.
